name: Strategic Positioning

positioning.primary_position: A private-first trip coordination hub with public support and receipt-backed spending transparency.
positioning.validated_wedge: External-provider public trip support plus member-only planning, receipt localization, and subgroup expense separation.

architecture_implications: Public pages must be a bounded surface, not a projection of the full private trip.
Public contribution checkout and payout must be delegated to an external crowdfunding/payment provider in MVP.
Contribution ledger, trip expense ledger, and private event ledger must be modeled separately.
Receipt records need both immutable source values and localized display views.
Private event payment status should remain tracking-only for MVP.
stage_3_focus: ExternalContributionProvider boundary and contribution-reference audit model
RBAC and visibility model across public, trip member, private event participant, and admin scopes
Receipt source-of-truth plus localization display model
Moderation and reporting hooks for public surfaces
recommendations: Advance architecture with an external-provider contribution boundary; do not build Crewly-owned pooled payments in MVP.
Do not merge private event balances into trip totals in MVP.
Make privacy defaults and public promotion flows explicit inputs to Stage 4 UX architecture.
